title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts control characters in connection fields
summary: Oracle MySQL host, database, and user fields did not reject control characters.
observed: Connection field values containing newline-style control characters could pass validation.
expected: Connection field values must be non-empty plain strings without control characters.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for connection-field control characters.
impact: Control characters can make diagnostics misleading and complicate safe logging of configuration errors.
delivery_semantics: The fix is configuration validation only and does not change committed-write behavior.
security: Rejecting control characters reduces log injection and configuration confusion risk.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL host, database, and user values reject control characters.
Normal non-empty values continue to validate.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
